place: Bloomingdale borough, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: Bloomingdale’s name means “valley of flowers,” a nod to the Dutch settlers who first called the area that in the 1700s.
The borough officially became independent on February 23, 1918 when Pompton Township was divided into three new municipalities.
The New Jersey Midland Railroad ran tracks through the area, placing a Bloomingdale station in what today is Riverdale.
The Bloomingdale Cornet Band has performed continuously since 1884, while Donald School, built in 1886, remains a historic landmark.
Dutch farmers settled the area in the mid‑1700s, moving west from Pompton Plains to farm the fertile valley that would become Bloomingdale.
Federal Hill in Bloomingdale was the site of the 1781 Pompton Mutiny, a winter revolt of Continental Army troops quelled by General Howe.
